name: binwalk3
title: binwalk3
summary: tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
description:
tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code
Binwalk is a tool for identifying, and optionally extracting, files and data
that have been embedded inside of other files.
.
While its primary focus is firmware analysis, it supports a wide variety of
file and data types.
.
Through entropy analysis, it can even help to identify unknown compression or
encryption!
.
Binwalk can be customized and integrated into your own Rust projects
.
This is an updated version of the Binwalk firmware analysis tool, re-written
in Rust for speed and accuracy.

commands.name: binwalk3
commands.description: Analyzes data for embedded file types

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use binwalk3 because tool library for analyzing binary blobs and executable code.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use binwalk3 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `binwalk3 [options] [args]` as documented upstream for binwalk3.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair binwalk3 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `binwalk3 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
